businessSummary.oneLiner: Leading AI data analytics platform for government and enterprise, built on three pillars: Gotham (defense), Foundry (commercial), AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue reached $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue topped $650M in Q4 (+137% YoY). Pentagon designated Maven AI as an official program of record, deploying it across the entire US military. FY2026 guidance of $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%) signals accelerating growth.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) — large-scale data integration
AIP (AI Platform) — generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps — rapid customer conversion programs

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme even for software. Rule of 40 score of 127% partially justifies but growth deceleration risk is significant

keyMetrics.name: P/S
keyMetrics.interpretation: 3-4x the SaaS average (10-15x). Only justified by Palantir's 60%+ growth rate

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2025)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Rare at this scale. Q4 grew 70% YoY

keyMetrics.name: US Commercial Revenue (Q4)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Explosive enterprise customer acquisition reducing government dependency

keyMetrics.name: Rule of 40
keyMetrics.interpretation: Revenue growth + profit margin = 127%. Elite among SaaS companies

keyMetrics.name: Net Dollar Retention
keyMetrics.interpretation: Existing customers spending 39% more YoY. Proves high product stickiness

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI designated US military program of record
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon officially designated Maven Smart System as program of record (3/20). Long-term military-wide deployment confirmed. Combat-tested in Iran with thousands of strikes

growthDrivers.title: UK FCA 3-month trial contract
growthDrivers.description: UK Financial Conduct Authority adopting Foundry on 3-month trial at £30K+/week. Signals expansion beyond defense into regulatory domains

growthDrivers.title: Combat-tested in Iran: thousands of strikes
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon using Palantir + Anthropic AI for target identification in Iran operations. Compresses strike cycle from hours to minutes

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 revenue guidance $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management confident in growth acceleration. US commercial becoming core growth engine

growthDrivers.title: US commercial revenue Q4 +137% explosion
growthDrivers.description: Q4 US commercial $650M (+137% YoY). 180 deals over $1M, 61 deals over $10M. AIP bootcamps driving rapid customer conversion

growthDrivers.title: Wedbush Dan Ives maintains $230 target
growthDrivers.description: Calls Palantir 'the backbone of US military AI' following Maven designation

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI, top-level security clearances, Maven program of record, Iran combat validation. Moats money can't buy

moats.description: Deeply integrated Ontology platform in government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high
Top-level clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
AIP bootcamps for rapid customer acquisition and expanding Ontology data model ecosystem
Real-time use in thousands of Iran strikes. Battle-tested credibility is an irreplaceable asset

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. $60B valuation round in progress. Hardware-focused, complementary to Palantir's software

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platforms but far behind in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering gov AI but lack Palantir's specialized Ontology integration depth

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform significantly behind in revenue and growth

recentNews.headline: PLTR -3.77% amid broad tech weakness
recentNews.significance: Despite Maven news, macro weakness and valuation concerns drove decline. Anthropic agent announcement pressured SaaS sector

recentNews.headline: PLTR surges +6.78% on Maven designation
recentNews.significance: Pentagon's official program of record designation triggered sharp rally

recentNews.headline: Pentagon designates Maven AI as official program of record
recentNews.significance: Upgraded from pilot to permanent program. Stable long-term budget allocation confirmed

recentNews.headline: UK FCA begins Foundry trial
recentNews.significance: Financial regulation expansion signals customer diversification beyond defense

recentNews.headline: Palantir + Anthropic AI used for Iran strike targeting
recentNews.significance: Maven system with Anthropic Claude supporting thousands of strikes. Combat validation strengthens future contract bids

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush Dan Ives maintains $230 target, 'backbone of US military AI.' Maven designation strengthens thesis. Multiple analysts flag P/E 100x as key risk

risks.title: Extreme valuation (Forward P/E ~100x)
risks.description: P/E 100x, P/S 44x are extreme. Any growth deceleration could trigger sharp correction

risks.title: Ethical controversy (Iran war AI use)
risks.description: Reports of Palantir + Anthropic AI used in Iran strikes. ESG investor departure and regulatory risk

risks.title: Anthropic 'adversary designation' controversy
risks.description: Palantir's AI partner Anthropic facing controversy, creating partner risk

risks.title: Broad tech weakness (macro)
risks.description: Oil above $100, Middle East tensions, Nasdaq near correction (-10% from highs). High-valuation stocks hit hardest

risks.title: Competition intensifying (Anduril $60B raise)
risks.description: Anduril pursuing $60B valuation funding round. Defense AI market competition accelerating

buyReasons.title: Maven program of record secures defense revenue base
buyReasons.rationale: Upgraded to permanent program. Long-term budget allocation and military-wide deployment confirmed

buyReasons.title: US commercial revenue +137% explosive growth
buyReasons.rationale: Q4 US commercial $650M. Diversifying beyond government while accelerating growth

buyReasons.title: Rule of 40 score 127%
buyReasons.rationale: Revenue growth + margin = 127%. Among the top SaaS companies globally

buyReasons.title: Combat-validated AI platform
buyReasons.rationale: Thousands of strikes supported in real combat. Data and trust that competitors can't replicate

overallOpinion: Maven's program of record designation elevated Palantir's defense moat to a new level. Combat use in thousands of Iran strikes is an asset no competitor can buy. UK FCA trial shows expansion potential beyond defense.
However, March 24's -3.77% drop reminds us of the Forward P/E 100x burden and broad tech weakness. Anthropic's agent capability announcement pressured the entire SaaS sector.

Trading above both 50-day ($148) and 200-day ($120) SMAs maintains bullish structure. Consensus $196 (+27%). Sufficient rebound potential when macro stabilizes, but new entry at P/E 100x carries significant risk.

investmentVerdict.summary: Maven designation and 60% growth are impressive but Forward P/E 100x makes fresh entry risky. Existing holders should maintain positions; consider adding on dips like 3/24. Monitor Iran-related ethical controversies.

sources.name: Motley Fool
sources.description: Maven designation + Iran combat use analysis

x.hook: Maven becomes official US military program, yet PLTR drops -3.77%
x.text:
$PLTR (3/25) Pentagon made Maven AI an official program of record. Deployed in thousands of Iran strikes. Yet -3.77% on 3/24. $154.78.

1/4
thread:
3/23: +6.78% on Maven news. 3/24: -3.77% on tech weakness. P/E 100x giveth and taketh away.

2/4

UK FCA trial, US commercial +137%, revenue guidance $7.2B (+61%). Growth is undeniable at any price.

3/4

Target $196 (+27%). Above both SMAs — bullish structure intact. If you can handle P/E 100x, it's a great company.

4/4

threads.hook: Maven becomes US military standard, stock surges then drops
threads.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $154.78 (-3.77%)

Pentagon designated Maven AI as an official program of record. Used in thousands of Iran strikes. Stock surged +6.78% on 3/23 then fell -3.77% on 3/24 amid tech weakness.

UK FCA started a Foundry trial. US commercial revenue +137% in Q4. Revenue guidance: $7.2B (+61%). Growth metrics are elite.

The issue is P/E 100x. Good news gets priced in, then macro pulls it back. Target $196 (+27%). Both SMAs still supportive. Structure is healthy — it's the price that's demanding.

witty.hook: Palantir wins the real war but loses the stock market battle
witty.text:
$PLTR (3/25) Supporting thousands of strikes in Iran, Pentagon makes it official. Stock? Down 3.77%. P/E 100x problems.

1/4
thread:
Winning the war, losing the Nasdaq. When tech sells off, P/E 100x stocks take the first hit.

2/4

Even the UK's financial regulator is a customer now. Commercial +137%. This growth rate at P/E 100x is... still expensive.

3/4

Target $196 (+27%). Wedbush says $230. The company's doing everything right — the price just already reflects 'very right.'

4/4

$PLTR (3/25) Up 6.78% on Maven news, down 3.77% the next day. The rollercoaster never stops. $154.78.

1/4

The company is doing everything right: Maven, FCA trial, +137% commercial growth. It's the valuation that makes every day stressful.

fact.hook: Maven program of record, surge then pullback
fact.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $154.78 (-3.77%)

Maven designated program of record. Iran combat-tested. 3/23: +6.78%, 3/24: -3.77%. FCA trial, commercial +137%, guidance $7.2B (+61%). Above both SMAs. Target $196 (+27%).

witty.hook: Winning real wars, losing the stock market war
witty.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $154.78 (-3.77%)

Palantir supported thousands of Iran strikes and got the Pentagon's official seal of approval. The stock's response? Up 6.78%, then down 3.77%. Net gain: some whiplash.

UK's financial regulator is a customer. Commercial revenue +137%. But P/E 100x means every good news day gets followed by a 'wait, is it still too expensive?' day.

Target $196 (+27%). Great company at an ambitious price. The question isn't whether Palantir delivers — it's whether it delivers enough to justify 100x.

empathy.hook: The PLTR rollercoaster — up big, down fast, repeat
empathy.text:
$PLTR (3/25) $154.78 (-3.77%)

Maven gets official designation, stock jumps 6.78%. Next day, tech sells off, stock drops 3.77%. If you're a PLTR holder, you know this dance well.

The company is executing brilliantly: combat-validated AI, FCA trial, commercial +137%, $7.2B guidance. The frustration isn't about the company — it's about the P/E 100x amplifying every market mood swing.

Above both SMAs. Target $196 (+27%). You're holding a great business. These dips test conviction, and so far, Palantir keeps giving reasons to stay.
